commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
And did. Hebrew, "and corrupted themselves. "Septuagint, "were more depraved than "
By which, is put instead of the Hebrew, "their stubborn (or hard) (Haydock) Chaldean, 'corrupt' way. "This hard and rough path denotes the labours which the wicked have to encounter, in the pursuit of pleasure, as they themselves confess. We wearied ourselves in the way of iniquity. And have walked through hard ways, Wisdom v. 7. (Calmet)
Though the life of the libertine seem delightful, it draws on the most serious evils and provokes the anger of God. (Menochius)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
